Container.Name: Matrix
Container.Overview:
All-in-one Matrix homeserver for Unraid: Synapse + coturn + Element Web (/element/) + Synapse-Admin (/admin/). Plug-and-play — just fill in SERVER_NAME and Postgres connection, everything else is generated automatically.
PREREQUISITES (before starting the container):
1) PostgreSQL database — connect to your Postgres container and run: CREATE USER matrix WITH PASSWORD 'yourpass'; CREATE DATABASE matrix ENCODING 'UTF8' LC_COLLATE='C' LC_CTYPE='C' TEMPLATE template0 OWNER matrix; (UTF8 + LC_COLLATE='C' is mandatory — Synapse will refuse to start otherwise.)
2) Reverse proxy (Nginx Proxy Manager) for matrix.yourdomain.tld → Unraid-IP:8008. In the proxy host → Advanced tab, add: client_max_body_size 100m; proxy_read_timeout 600s; (Without these, media uploads fail and long-running requests time out.)
3) For federation: two extra Custom Locations on the SAME proxy host for /.well-known/matrix/server and /.well-known/matrix/client → Unraid-IP:8080. Full step-by-step guide (NPM screenshots, well-known, first user): https://github.com/junkerderprovinz/matrix#readme
Container.Requires: An external PostgreSQL database (UTF8, LC_COLLATE=C) and a reverse proxy (e.g. Nginx Proxy Manager) with large-upload and long-timeout settings. See the documentation for the exact setup: https://github.com/junkerderprovinz/matrix#readme

Config.Name: Matrix HTTP API
Config.Description: Synapse HTTP API. Expose via reverse proxy with HTTPS — never directly to the internet.

Config.Name: Element Web + Synapse-Admin
Config.Description: Serves Element Web (/element/), Synapse-Admin (/admin/) and /.well-known/matrix/* endpoints.

Config.Name: TURN TCP
Config.Description: TURN/STUN over TCP for voice/video calls. Open this port in your router/firewall.

Config.Name: TURN UDP
Config.Description: TURN/STUN over UDP for voice/video calls. Open this port in your router/firewall.

Config.Name: TURN TLS TCP
Config.Description: TURN over TLS (TCP). Optional — only if TLS certs are mounted.

Config.Name: TURN TLS UDP
Config.Description: TURN over TLS (UDP). Optional — only if TLS certs are mounted.

Config.Name: TURN relay ports (UDP)
Config.Description: UDP relay port range for TURN (voice/video calls). Must match the container's coturn min-port/max-port.

Config.Name: Prometheus Metrics
Config.Description: Prometheus metrics at /_synapse/metrics. Bind to a private network only.

Config.Name: Data Directory
Config.Description: Persistent data: homeserver.yaml, media, signing keys. Never delete — destroys keys and breaks federation.

Config.Name: TURN-TLS Certs
Config.Description: Optional. Mount a folder containing fullchain.pem + privkey.pem to enable TURN-TLS on 5349.

Config.Name: Matrix Server Domain
Config.Description: Your Matrix domain, e.g. matrix.yourdomain.tld. Used in Matrix IDs (@user:matrix.yourdomain.tld). Cannot be changed later without wiping the DB.

Config.Name: Anonymous Usage Reporting
Config.Description: Send anonymous usage stats to Matrix.org. 'yes' or 'no'. Recommended: no.

Config.Name: Enable Federation
Config.Description: true = chat with the public Matrix network. The container now serves /.well-known/matrix/* itself (serve_server_wellknown) as long as https://SERVER_NAME/ is routed to Synapse — no extra proxy rule needed. false = private island.

Config.Name: Enable Registration
Config.Description: false = accounts created by the admin only (recommended). true = open self-service signup WITHOUT verification (shows Element's Create Account button) — only enable behind a CAPTCHA or on a trusted network, or you will get spam signups.

Config.Name: TURN Domain
Config.Description: Optional. Public hostname clients reach TURN (voice/video) at. Leave empty to use SERVER_NAME; set a dedicated subdomain (e.g. turn.yourdomain.tld) to route TURN around a reverse proxy.

Config.Name: TURN Port
Config.Description: Optional. Public TURN port advertised to clients (default 3478). Change only if you remapped the published TURN port.

Config.Name: PostgreSQL Host
Config.Description: IP of your PostgreSQL server (e.g. Unraid host IP). 192.168.1.10 is a placeholder — replace it with your server's IP. IP+port is more reliable than container names.

Config.Name: PostgreSQL Port
Config.Description: PostgreSQL port. Default 5432.

Config.Name: PostgreSQL Username
Config.Description: Postgres user for Synapse. Must already exist (see README — Postgres setup).

Config.Name: PostgreSQL Password
Config.Description: Password for the Postgres user above.

Config.Name: PostgreSQL Database
Config.Description: Synapse database name. Must be created with ENCODING UTF8, LC_COLLATE='C', LC_CTYPE='C' — see Overview.

Config.Name: Matrix Admin Username (Element / Synapse-Admin login)
Config.Description: Optional. Localpart of the admin user to auto-create on first boot (e.g. 'admin' → @admin:SERVER_NAME). Logs into Element Web + Synapse-Admin. Clear after creation.

Config.Name: Matrix Admin Password (Element / Synapse-Admin login)
Config.Description: Optional. Password for the admin user above. Clear after first boot.

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Container timezone (affects log timestamps).

Config.Name: User ID (PUID)
Config.Description: UID Synapse runs as. Default 99 (nobody on Unraid).

Config.Name: Group ID (PGID)
Config.Description: GID Synapse runs as. Default 100 (users on Unraid).
